Nancy Hennessy is murdered and leaves behind a secret: The identity of a lover who nobody knew about. Not her husband. Not her friends. Not her daughter. The adaptation is soon to air on AppleTV+ starring Elisabeth Moss and Kerry Washington.
Best Drama
Where the Wildflowers Grow: A Novel
Be inspired by Terah Shelton Harris' newest story of survival and redemption.

"Where the Wildflowers Grow" opens with a harrowing scene: A prison bus crashes over a ravine leaving only one survivor. Leigh survives and runs away to a flower farm in rural Alabama, starting a tale of hope and redemption.
Best Feel-Good Read
That's What Friends Are For
Inspired by The Golden Girls, this hilarious story celebrates love, aging, and finding your people.

Fans of "The Golden Girls" will love this book inspired by the show about friendship and aging in style. Theodore Copeland's estranged sister and her granddaughter show up at his oasis in Palm Springs, bringing secrets and disruption to his life with the "Golden Gays" (his nickname for his friend group).
Best Binge Read
Python's Kiss: Stories
Explore Louise Erdrich’s writing with this magnificent collection of her stories.

Louise Erdrich, whose last book was chosen for Read With Jenna's book club, has a new collection of short stories written from the past two decades of her storied career. According to the description from the publisher, the story collection features characters like "a tribal newsletter editor whose son tells her a story that nothing in her experience can encompass" and a girl who decides to spend her life with a stone.

title: Are Alex Henderson and Ashley Carpenter from ‘Love Is Blind’ Still Together Now?
summary: Season 10 stars Alex or Ashley had lingering tension between them ever since they left the pods. Find out if they said “I do” on their wedding day in Episode 12.
story: The teaser for the weddings episode of “Love Is Blind” Season 10 included quick snippets from multiple nuptials to make viewers question which couples said “I do” and which called it quits. Most of the footage didn’t give too much away, but one moment did: “She flat out called him a liar.”

That quote wasn’t attributed to a specific person in the subtitles, but the following scene showed contestant Ashley Carpenter wiping away tears, signaling that the heated moment would involve her relationship with Alex Hendrerson.

At the start of Season 10, viewers met Alex and Ashley in the pods. Alex, a former professional soccer player, explained that he wanted to go on the show to challenge himself to stop prioritizing physical attraction. Ashley, on the other hand, said she had “a bad picker” in the past but was hoping the experiment would change that pattern.

The two fell in love and got engaged. The chemistry between them seemed palpable the moment they met in person, but Alex revealed in a confessional interview that Ashely wasn’t his usual type. From then on, there appeared to be lingering tension between them. Alex received a “pretty aggressive” grilling from Ashley’s dad, which he spoke to TODAY.com about, where he answered questions about his past occupations, upbringing and nomadic lifestyle. Later, Ashley and Alex argued about where they would live in the future and he pondered if she would use unscrupulous means to get him to stay in Ohio and not relocate to his dream destinations of Arizona or Florida.

Then, the trust between them was further damaged when Ashley learned at the pod squad meet-up from another contestant that Alex previously lived with his ex-girlfriend in Austin shortly before coming onto the show. But Ashely and Alex enjoyed a relaxing final date and both said they were looking forward to the wedding.
Were their nuptials drama-free? Read on to find out if Alex or Ashley said “I do” on their wedding day.
Did Alex and Ashley Get Married in the 'Love Is Blind' Finale?

Ashley reflected on her past relationships shortly after arriving at the wedding venue. She said she had been cheated on and lied to but being with Alex made her feel confident. She praised him for overcoming soccer injuries and humble beginnings.

In his groom suite, Alex also gushed about his fiancée. He said she checked all his boxes and was “a top-tier partner when it comes to choosing a wife.” He told his friends that he wasn’t feeling nervous before comparing his wedding day to his energy before a soccer game.
“I’ve been here before,” he said, adding that he wasn't “emotionally numb.”

While preparing to walk down the aisle, Ashley’s tone changed. “There have been many conversations that I’m not Alex’s type. So I hope when he sees me today he realizes how wrong he is,” she said. She revealed she would be “completely shocked” if Alex said he wanted to marry her. She added that she would be happy, but she was “expecting” him to reject her.
“Someone out there is going to be the love of my life,” she said, hinting at the scathing speech she would soon deliver in front of their friends and family.

During their vows, Alex said he enjoyed getting to know Ashley and fell for her sense of humor. She described their journey as a “crazy roller coaster” and said he became her best friend the moment he entered the pods.

When the officiant asked Alex to reveal his decision, Ashley interrupted him. “I know your answer, and it doesn’t matter to me. I know you don’t choose me, and I’m not choosing you either,” she told him.

She continued, “When we were in the pods, all of our stories, all of our future plans, they aligned perfectly. However, once we left the pods, there was no effort. There was no intimacy. There were no questions you had for me. I felt like as soon as we moved into the apartment, we were roommates.”

The venue remained completely silent as Ashley further explained her reasoning. “Something always kind of felt off. Your stories were so vivid. They were so detailed. You would say a lot of words but somehow I always found myself more confused at the end of our conversations. Lying is a non-negotiable for me. I’m sure it is for you as well. I lead my life with integrity, which is one of the reasons you’ve told me you fell in love with me. I expect my husband to do the same thing,” she said.

Ashley said in the past she used to ignore red flags and accept dishonesty, but she has grown during the experiment. “I deserve so much better. So I am so sorry, but it is a ‘no’ from me today,” she concluded.

After her speech, Alex said he respected her decision. “I do disagree to an extent about the integrity,” he replied. He also pushed back on her concerns about his past, saying that he felt like her questions “were more to try and catch me in something than really understand.”
“From my end, it’s mutual,” he said.
But Ashley got the final word. “Saying ‘no’ to you today is a win,” she said before walking away.
Alex’s mom, Kristin, and his friends went to support him. His mom wasn’t happy that Ashley “flat out called him a liar,” as shown in the preview.

But he assured them there was no ill will between them. However, he told the cameras that Ashley’s speech was a “power move” and that her inquiries about his past was her looking for a way out. He also cryptically said there are things in her past he could’ve mentioned.
Meanwhile, Ashley said in her confessional she was sure Alex was bothered by her integrity comment. “I don’t care. I just don’t care. Today was all about me,” she said.
She added, while chatting with her friends, “He’s a loser…He didn’t love me. He didn’t even like me I don’t think.”
In her final confessional, she revealed, “I think he wanted something else out of this rather than a wife.”

What more do the two of them have to reveal? Based on the fiery trailer for the reunion, out March 11, there is plenty more drama to unpack. Tune in to see what Ashley and Alex have to say about their relationship, and who else from the season is called out.

title: Are Amber Morrison and Jordan Faeth from 'Love Is Blind' Still Together Now?
summary: Most "Love Is Blind" contestants enter the dating experiment looking for their soulmate, but the stakes have always been higher for single parents who take part in the reality series
story: Most "Love Is Blind" contestants enter the dating experiment looking for their soulmate, but the stakes have always been higher for single parents who take part in the reality series.

While speaking to single men in the Season 10 dating pods, Amber Morrison was upfront about having a 7-year-old daughter. And she quickly formed a connection with Jordan Faeth, whose dealbreaker going into the experiment, ironically, was dating someone with a child.
Love blossomed for the couple and they moved in together after getting engaged sight-unseen. Still, Jordan was eager and anxious to meet Amber's daughter.
Did the couple end up saying "I do" on decision day? We're taking a look back at their love journey.
What Happened Between Amber and Jordan in the Pods?
When Amber, a 34-year-old nurse practitioner, started talking with Jordan, a 34-year-old account executive, they both started catching feelings pretty quickly.
After learning that Amber had a daughter, Jordan kept an open mind, even though he didn't go into the dating experiment looking for someone with a child.

"When I was first interviewing for 'Love Is Blind,' they asked me about dealbreakers and I said having a kid was a dealbreaker. And now, here we are. So this has been something I've been thinking a lot about. It's probably why I'm not getting much sleep at night. But she's been telling me so much about Emma and I'm like, now it's kind of exciting. I think that I'm a very lucky guy to be in this position at all and I hope that she feels the same," he said.
The couple fostered a judgment-free environment while discussing a range of topics, including finances, during their dates. They also agreed that they matched each other's level of weirdness.
"I just think I am weird and I'm OK with that," Amber said.
"Yeah, I mean it'd be really nice to have somebody to be weird with," Jordan said.
In Episode 2, Amber said Jordan was beginning to feel like "home" for her in a confessional interview.

Jordan was still a bit nervous about being a father figure to Amber's daughter, but he started to look at it as more of a "privilege" and loosened up a bit knowing that she already has a strong relationship with her father.
In Episode 2, the couple got engaged. Before meeting in person, Amber reflected on their growing bond in a confessional interview.
"I have chosen Jordan to be my husband because he is genuine, caring, loving — what I wanted in a man," she said.
Amber went on to share one of the things she loved the most about getting to know Jordan.
"Having somebody say that I'm enough based on the human being that I am, it's incredible," she said.
Meanwhile, Jordan said he was feeling "so good" and "so confident" about his choice to get engaged to Amber.
"She's super caring. She's an amazing role model for her daughter," he said.
What Happened When Amber and Jordan Moved in Together?

After returning home from Mexico, Amber and Jordan gave each other a tour of their homes. They agreed that they both wanted to live somewhat frugally and not live beyond their means when it comes to purchasing a future home together.
Jordan didn't meet Amber's daughter right away, but he did meet her daughter's father and it went well.

Things seemed to be going smoothly until the couple met up with their castmates one night and Amber overheard Jordan talking to one of his co-stars about how he hadn't met Emma yet. Jordan seemed enthused about the future encounter, but admitted that it all was a little "scary."
Amber then addressed Jordan and said he could "bail" at any point if he wanted to. He quickly reassured her that he had no plans of doing so.
The moment led Amber to pull away from Jordan the next day, which only escalated the situation.

“This is definitely the first argument that we’ve gotten in and I wouldn’t say it was good. I definitely get needing to take a beat and like go take a breather cause that’s how I am a lot of times, I have  to cool my head. So that I understand. but the total silence is I mean you can’t work with that," he said.
When Amber returned home, she told Jordan she understood that he might be a little nervous to meet her daughter, but questioned why he spoke about it with his co-stars instead of her.

“I think you’re incredible. I don’t think I’ve ever felt this way about anybody before. The only reason I’ve gone this far is because of you. But before, you know, making a lifelong commitment, I have to meet Emma first. Never meeting her before and trying to build a relationship, it's a scary thought," Jordan said.
Jordan went on to explain that things had "gotten more real" in the last week and he was beginning to feel nervous that Emma wouldn't like him.
The couple smoothed things over and agreed that they would work together to ensure that the meeting with Emma went well.
Did Amber and Jordan Get Married in the 'Love Is Blind' Finale?
On her wedding day, Amber was confident in her decision to say "I do" at the altar, but acknowledged that she understood if it was too much for Jordan to become a husband and stepfather all at once.
The moment occurred when Amber was talking with her brother Jacob before walking down the aisle.
"I'm personally expecting this not to go through just because I think that, you know, maybe he wants more time," Jacob said.
"For me, it's a yes but that's a lot to ask for him. I'm understanding either way, though," Amber replied.
While getting ready, Jordan was visibly nervous for the moment of truth.
At the altar, Amber was first to make her decision and said, "I do, but I understand if there's any doubts. I have a lot going on."
When it was Jordan's turn, he took a leap of faith.
"I want to follow my heart and say that I do," he said.
In a confessional interview after the ceremony, Amber expressed her excitement over Jordan's decision, saying, "I was fully expecting him to not be ready for this. I was not expecting that at all."
Jordan then explained that he got some important advice from his sister before walking down the aisle.
"My sister told me, 'Just follow your heart.' So I did," he said.

title: Are Brittany Wicker and Devonta Anderson from 'Love Is Blind' Still Together Now?
summary: “Love Is Blind” Season 10 stars Brittany Wicker and Devonta Anderson still together now? Find out if they got married in the wedding episode finale.
story: “Love Is Blind” Season 10 stars Brittany Wicker and Devonta Anderson have had an unusual journey on the show compared to the other six couples who got engaged this season.

For starters, the show didn’t introduce the couple and their love story until Episode 3. It was then that viewers learned Brittany had already purchased a wedding dress before coming onto the show as a way of “manifesting” encountering her dream man in the pods. She met Devonta and the two formed a strong connection that grew when he shared his grandfather died. He decided to stay on the show to find the love of his life. He later proposed to Brittany and she happily accepted.

But their dynamic changed the instant they met face to face. Brittany acknowledged that Devonta wasn’t her typical type, and on their honeymoon trip Devonta was more reserved and less affectionate than expected. It appeared the two were going to call it quits after a rocky start, but they continued on with the experiment.

Fans watched the couple move in together, Devonta meet and bond with Brittany’s family and the reveal of her dream wedding dress. However, their obstacles remained, with Devonta not providing enough words of affirmation and Brittany being hesitant to sign a prenuptial agreement.
How did their journey on the show end? Find out what happened on Brittany and Devonta’s wedding day below.
Did Brittany and Devonta Get Married?

As always, every nuptials on “Love Is Blind” begins with a title card dedicated to the wedding. So when the text “Brittany & Devonta Wedding Day” appeared on the screen, it seemed like it was going to be business as usual. Txhat wasn’t the case.
After showing an empty wedding venue, cameras cut to Brittany and Devonta having a conversation in an apartment.
“I love you. I see life with you,” Brittany said to her fiancé. “I want to be your wife. I’m shocked that we’re not getting married.”

In her confessional, Brittany provided more details. She explained that the day was supposed to be their wedding day and all her family came into town to celebrate the planned nuptials. She said learning she wasn’t going to walk down the aisle was “kind of shocking.”

Devonta, who remained silent while Brittany recapped the day, agreed that everything had been fine between them. However, he added that he “can’t walk into a marriage with self-doubt of, ‘Can I love you how you need to be love?’”

He said those doubts prevented him from going through with the wedding. While his language was more uncertain, Brittany was definitive. She said she still sees a life with him and that even though they aren’t saying “I do” right now it doesn’t mean they won’t get married eventually. Devonta simply nodded in response.

“I feel like we’re still together, and we’re just taking a slight pause on when we actually get married,” she explained. She also mentioned her willingness to sign the prenup despite being against it as proof that their connection was strong.
She emphasized, “We’re still going to be together. We’re still going to be engaged. I do see 100% marrying each other. Our story’s not over.”
While her confessionals featured footage of her talking about having children with Devonta, his interviews showed him being apprehensive.

“I’m not a fortune teller so I don’t know. There’s still doubts that I have, but it’s like, I don’t know the answer ... I don’t know what the future holds,” he said. He also voiced one of his concerns: Brittany pre-buying her wedding dress.
He said when he learned she had already purchased her gown prior to meeting him, it “threw” him.
“‘Were you going to get married regardless if it was me?’” he wondered.
Meanwhile, Brittany said she was now 99% sure they would tie the knot one day.

Devonta then revealed more issues in their relationship in his confessional. He described Brittany as a “shadow” and said sometimes he needs space. He said he doesn’t want to keep “dragging” their relationship if he knows it isn’t going to work out.
“We will be in touch,” he told Brittany before leaving.
She laughed off the remark but told the cameras that she had been worrying she had stronger feelings for him than he did for her. She revealed that he never introduced her to his mom.
Brittany also said she was fearful of the relationship not working out and having to start over again.
What Has Brittany Said About Her Relationship with Devonta?
In an interview with TODAY.com prior to the wedding episode being released, Brittany spoke about her relationship with Devonta.
She said she decided to continue with the experiment even though they struggled early on because she felt like the person she fell in love with in the pods was still there.

“He was just processing a lot outside of having a new fiancée. He had a lot going on at home with his family, and he was verbally telling me, ‘I want to be in this,’” she recalled. “So for me, I had to make a decision. Am I going to trust this man and give us a real chance, or am I just going to give up on us? To me, I didn’t want to give up on us, because in a marriage you don’t give up on someone, and the end goal was to get married.”
Brittany said she chose to trust Devonta despite being “hesitant.”
She revealed that she and Devonta both share the same love language — physical touch — which is why she mentioned multiple times that she expected him to be more affectionate.

“That’s why I was having such a hard time understanding why he didn’t want to touch me and why I felt so disconnected. Because I’m like, it’s not even like we have to meet in the middle on it. It is both of our number one love language, and it’s just not making sense why it wasn’t connecting,” she explained.
She told TODAY.com that she had conversations with Devonta off camera about his lack of physical touch.

“He basically explained that when he is in a place of feeling a lot of stress and pressure and having to go home, he doesn’t want to be touched. So that’s why his love language was not being reciprocated at that time. All I needed was for him to just tell that to me,” she said.

“Because then I’m not going to be offended that you don’t want me to touch you, because you’re going through it, and right now you just don’t want to be touched. You will want to be touched. It’s just in this moment that’s not how he wanted to receive love.”

title: Are Emma Betsinger and Mike Gibney from ‘Love Is Blind’ Still Together Now?
summary: Are Season 10 Love Is Blind stars Emma and Mike still together? Find out what happened in the finale and if they got married in the wedding episode.
story: During Season 10 of “Love Is Blind,” fans watched one couple navigate a relationship dilemma that hadn’t been deeply explored on the show previously: whether or not to have a family.

When contestant Emma Betsinger first introduced herself at the start of the season, she explained that she was adopted when she was a child by a white family and never learned the identity of her birth parents. She also revealed that she had 10 surgeries before the age of 7 because she was born with birthmarks all over her body that had “a very high chance of melanoma.”

Since Emma didn’t know the medical history of her birth parents, she worried about having kids and possibly passing down a trait that would cause them to go through the same health struggles she faced. So, after sharing her background with Mike Gibney in the pods, she clarified that she wasn’t sure she wanted to have children.

Despite Mike always dreaming of becoming a dad, the two fell for each other and got engaged. But their different plans for their future continued to be an unsettled conversation between them as the show progressed.
Were they finally able to get on the same page when they faced each other at the altar? Find out what happened on Emma and Mike’s wedding day below.
Did Emma and Mike Get Married in the 'Love Is Blind' Finale?
A majority of Emma and Mike’s wedding day was shown in Episode 11, which was included in the latest group of episodes out Feb. 25.

After arriving at the venue, Emma said she was in her head because she and Mike still hadn’t resolved their issues about having children. She said the two of them did not talk prior to the wedding and did not stay together the night before.
But, Emma still praised Mike and their connection. She became emotional as she expressed how “light, free, happy and comfortable” her fiancé made her feel.
Meanwhile, Mike arrived with nervous energy. While speaking with his parents, he said that he needed to hear Emma say “yes” at the altar to truly know that she wanted to have children with him.

At the altar, Mike said in his vows that although their journey hadn’t been easy, Emma was “open and understanding” throughout the entire process. He also repeated his concern about her wanting to start a family.
As for Emma, she said, “You have made me feel so special and empowered me to march to the beat of my own drum … I can’t ask for a better partner throughout my life.”

The officiant asked Emma to announce her decision first. She said that although there are problems they have to sort through, she did not want their relationship to end. “I’ve been thinking about how you make me feel and I can’t see a future without you. And I love you!” she gushed.
The episode ended on a cliffhanger. Episode 12, which was released March 4, picked up with Mike’s answer.
“Emma, I do love you,” he replied. “Right now, I’m not ready to say ‘I do.’”
He continued, “I want kids. I want to know that you definitely 100% want to have a family with me. I don’t want to say ‘yes’ and jump into something and then it change down the road.”
Mike apologized and, again, rehashed the ongoing conversation about children that they have been having since the pods.
After the ceremony, Mike went to check on Emma. She said that she saw “glimpses” of their future family which was why she wanted to marry him. He said he needed more certainty from her.
In her confessional, Emma said that love is a choice and she thought that sticking it out would be “enough” for Mike.

While speaking to a producer, he said that he would’ve changed his answer if he felt Emma actually wanted to have children. But, he said he was getting the impression that she didn’t want “the responsibility of having kids ever.”
The two will reunite to reflect on their differences and confirm where they stand today when the reunion episode arrives March 11.

title: Are Victor St. John and Christine Hamilton from 'Love Is Blind' Still Together Now?
summary: Did Victor St John and Christine Hamilton stay together after the Love Is Blind finale? Did they get married? Find out now.
story: “Love Is Blind” is known for creating long-lasting romantic connections and serving up plenty of drama. But one Season 10 couple has proven that the latter doesn’t necessarily have to be a part of the dating experiment.

Throughout Season 10, Victor “Vic” St. John and Christine Hamilton have shown viewers that it’s possible to navigate the highs and lows of the “Love Is Blind” journey with grace and respect for each other.
What made the couple fall for each other and did they end up getting married? We’re recapping their love story below.
What Happened Between Vic and Christine in the Pods?
Vic, a 34-year-old professor, and Christine, a 31-year-old speech-language pathologist, had instant chemistry when they first appeared in the dating pods.
The duo connected on their religious faith and shared experiences with distant or abusive fathers and stepfathers.
In a confessional, Vic expressed his gratitude for their budding relationship.
“I feel blessed that I found Christine in this experience. I’m in awe and I’m excited and I’m in love,” he said.
By the end of Episode 1, the couple was engaged. Before meeting her new fiancé, Christine reflected on her bond with Vic in a confessional interview.

“When two people love each other, you can’t stop it. I know him and I know his heart. And I really fully believe that he sees that in me too. And it’s the best feeling you’ve ever felt in your life times a million,” she said.
Meanwhile, Vic was excited to meet Christine in person after solely focusing on their emotional connection.

“For her to see my most vulnerable sides and some of my most vulnerable stories, and for her to still accept me for who I am and want to love me and want to have a forever future with me, there’s no words for it,” he said.
Why Didn’t Christine and Vic Go to Mexico With the Other Engaged Couples?

Christine and Vic were one of seven couples who got engaged in the “Love Is Blind” Season 10 dating pods. However, only six couples vacationed in Mexico together after meeting in person for the first time.

Instead of joining their castmates, Christine and Vic traveled to Malibu on their own, without a camera crew. They filmed highlights of their trip and reunited with the camera crew when they returned to Ohio.
In a statement to TODAY.com, “Love Is Blind” executive producer Chris Coelen explained why the couple went on a solo vacation.

“We’ve never had the resources to send more than six couples to Mexico, but this season, we felt so good about the couples that for the very first time we chose to follow seven of them, producing the biggest season in ‘Love Is Blind’ history! In order to make this work, we had to offer one couple the chance to go to Malibu on their own getaway,” Coelen said.
Since Vic and Christine didn’t have any unfinished business with other cast members they dated in the pods, producers decided that they should go on a trip by themselves.

“While the romantic getaways are a chance to not only see if the couples can turn their emotional connection into a physical one, they are also a chance to address outstanding feelings and issues with others they may have dated in the pods,” the statement continued.
What Happened When Vic and Christine Moved in Together?
Vic and Christine’s smooth journey continued when they moved in together and began meeting each other’s families.

Vic won the seal of approval from Christine’s mother, who offered the following reaction after meeting him: “Vic is very gentle. I like that about him. He seems very self-assured, confident. And I thought he was hot. Can I say that?”

As they got closer to decision day, Vic and Christine discussed their timeline for starting a family and agreed that they wanted to wait two years before looking into purchasing a home and having children.
Did Vic and Christine Get Married in the 'Love Is Blind' Finale?
Shortly before decision day, Vic and Christine had a romantic spa date and expressed their excitement for their future.
“I’ve been praying for you for a lot longer than I’ve known you,” Christine said.
Vic agreed and said, “I don’t think I’ve ever felt as confident about anything in my life, especially something so serious as this.”
While reflecting on the time they’d spent together so far, Vic said he sometimes gets “speechless” around his fiancée.
“I know you’ll make an excellent partner,” he said.
Christine said she was excited to call Vic her husband and the father of her children.
“I think we’re gonna complement each other and push each other. You push me to be better,” she said.

On their wedding day, the bride and groom exchanged their vows and reminisced on their whirlwind romance. They both said “I do” and celebrated their new adventure as husband and wife with their family and friends.
In a confessional interview after the  ceremony, Vic called the wedding the “best day of my life times 10.”
Christine also reflected on her new title: wife.
“Being married feels just like it should. It feels perfect,” she said.

title: Everyone's Talking About Benny Blanco and His Dirty Feet
summary: Benny Blanco and his feet have been making headlines for the past week and the saga is continuing.
story: Benny Blanco and his feet have been making headlines for the past week and the saga is continuing.

The music producer recently launched a new podcast with Lil Dicky, whose real name is David Burd, and his wife, Kristin Batalucco, called "Friends Keep Secrets." The trio had been teasing the release of their first episode, which arrived Feb. 24, across social media platforms. But when "Friends Keep Secrets" debuted, fans could only focus on one thing: Blanco's feet.

Just over a minute into the debut, Blanco lounged on a sofa beside Batalucco with his feet propped up, revealing his dirty soles. Social media users then began sharing screenshots of the moment, later leading to a response from Blanco and wife Selena Gomez.
Learn more about why the internet can't stop talking about Blanco and his controversial feet, below.
When and Why Were Benny Blanco's Feet Dirty?

About a minute into the first episode of Blanco's "Friends Keep Secrets" podcast, the music producer lifted his rear and farted. Somehow, that was not the moment that rubbed fans the wrong way. That came just a few seconds later when he placed his feet on the sofa, with dirt extending from his toes to his heels fully visible.
Those watching couldn't believe their eyes. One commented beneath the YouTube video, "Benny dawg wash your dawgs."
Another wrote, "Good podcast, wash your feet benny and wash the floors."
A third said, "Next time tell Benny to take his feet off before coming in the house. Lol them things were disgustingly dirty."
The conversation continued on X, with some describing his feet as "filthy."
What Did Benny Blanco Say About His Dirty Feet?
Blanco addressed his dirty feet during a “Jimmy Kimmel Live” interview that aired on Feb. 26 after the talk show host asked him about the moment.
“First of all, it’s the facility’s fault. It’s not mine,” Blanco said, before removing one shoe and sock to reveal his clean foot. “I have great feet.”
Blanco then continued to show off his clean feet as the camera zoomed in on them.
Lil Dicky, who was also a part of the interview, added that it was the first day they filmed their podcast, “So the crew was coming in and out.”
Host Jimmy Kimmel chimed in and asked, “So, you’re saying that Dave’s floors are not clean?”
Lil Dicky quipped, “On that day, it was dirty. You know how floors get.”
Selena Gomez Kisses Benny Blanco's Feet
For the second episode of “Friends Keep Secrets,” the trio invited Gomez to be their special guest.
Gomez opened up about a variety of topics, from her mental health to her friendship with Taylor Swift and being the inspiration behind the pop icon’s song “Dorothea.”

But toward the end of the episode, Gomez seemed to not so subtly respond to the criticism about her husband’s hygiene. When the co-hosts were chatting about children, Gomez leaned in and kissed her husband’s foot, which was noticeably less dirty.
“You like that?” Blanco asked his co-hosts with a cheeky smile on his face.
Gomez then playfully hit Blanco and said, “Oh, don’t make it a moment.”
He replied, “Oh no, I wasn’t. I liked it. It made me feel good. I love you so much.”
Gomez said, “I love you,” as she placed her head on her husband’s toes.
But social media users weren't swayed by her gesture.
“Opened this app to see selena kissing a dirty foot, and I think that’s me done for today,” one tweeted.
Another said, “I AM DISGUSTED.”

title: Rob Rausch Reveals If His Girlfriend Has Seen 'The Traitors'
summary: Has Rob Rausch's girlfriend seen The Traitors? He told TODAY.com about whether his girlfriend has seen his winning season.
story: At the end of "The Traitors" Season 4, high off his victory as one of the greatest Traitors the show has ever seen, Rob Rausch outlined his next steps: Find a girl who doesn't watch much TV and live a "simple life."
He's already made progress. During the reunion, Rob revealed he has a girlfriend of about two months.
But has she seen her boyfriend seemingly lie effortlessly, over and over, and come out on top over 20 competitors, some of them legends from "Big Brother" and "Survivor"?
Speaking to TODAY.com alongside fellow finalists Eric Nam and Maura Higgins, Rob confirms that his girlfriend has seen "The Traitors."
“And she feels bad for me,” Maura adds. “I like her. A lot.”
The "Love Island" alum made it to the very end with Rob, trusting him even as suspicion grew among other castmates (looking at you, Tara Lipinski).
Her expression of disbelief after learning he was playing her all along has become an unforgettable moment from the series.
Rob, 27, who is from Florence, Alabama, says he didn't go into the show necessarily expecting to win — "but I thought that I was capable of winning, for sure."
In the mornings, he would repeat the mantra, "I am a Faithful," to remind himself to act cool. He's said he modeled himself off of Maura, who hadn't seen the show and seemed bewildered at every turn.

"You don't understand — at the castle, the two of them were like, 'We don't know what we're doing. Why are we here? We've never seen this show.' She'd be like, 'Are people getting murdered every night?'" Eric Nam tells TODAY.com.
Eric says that's part of how Rob made it work: "(Rob and Maura were) just so under the radar for most of it."
So much so that when Eric was recruited to be a Traitor, he didn't expect Rob to be his counterpart.

"I was actually more afraid that it was Johnny (Weir), and then I was like, if it's Johnny, he's gonna get banished. And then I'm alone, and then I'm screwed, because I don't know what I'm doing. And then when I saw Rob, I was like, oh, he's killing it, so we're fine," Eric, who was ultimately betrayed by Rob at the final roundtable, says.
Each finalist says they are walking away from the experience with a different lesson. Maura says, "Don't trust men," while Eric takes a broader stroke: "Don't trust anyone."
As for Rob?
"Famous people are really easy to manipulate," he says. (Rob, who won $220,800 because of the “The Traitors,” is known for starring in “Love Island USA’s” explosively popular sixth season in 2024.)
"He just called us gullible again," Eric says.
"Again?" Maura adds.
"And famous," Rob clarifies.

title: Maura Higgins Shares What Really Happened After Rob Rausch's Reveal in 'The Traitors' Finale
summary: Maura Higgins went through “every single emotion” when her closest ally betrayed her — and it played out completely on camera in “The Traitors” Season 4.Maura made it to the final fire pit alongside her fellow “Love Island”-er Rob Rausch, with complete trust and a pinky promise on the line with her counterpart.
story: Maura Higgins went through “every single emotion” when her closest ally betrayed her — and it played out completely on camera in “The Traitors” Season 4.
Maura made it to the final fire pit alongside her fellow “Love Island”-er Rob Rausch, with complete trust and a pinky promise on the line with her counterpart.

So when he revealed that all along he had actually been playing the game as a Traitor — meaning he got to keep the entire $220,800 prize pot for himself instead of splitting it with her — her expression was of shock and heartbreak.

“I just kept waiting for him to say, ‘I’m joking,’” Maura tells TODAY.com in an interview alongside Rob and finalist Eric Nam less than a week after the finale and Season 4 reunion hit Peacock Feb. 26.
On the show, her processing was edited down to just a few minutes. In reality, it took longer, she and Rob say.

“I was in complete disbelief. I’m never speechless, like I always have something to say ... Then I realized, ‘OK, he’s not joking,’” Maura says. “Then I got a bit angry, and then maybe a bit sad, and then I was just like, ‘OK, well, fair play. Great game.’”
Some of that anger manifested with her calling Rob a “bad, bad man” and saying, “You’re never gonna have a girlfriend after this. You’re such a good liar.”
(Rob has since revealed that after filming, he did get a girlfriend, and she has seen his performance on “The Traitors.”)
There was also some name-calling viewers didn’t see, Maura says.
“Did I call you anymore names, at least?” she asks Rob, who nods. “What did I call you?”
“Um ... I don’t think it’s appropriate,” Rob responds.
But after the anger and sadness, Rob and Maura also “joked for a bit,” he says. “We were laughing.”
Before Maura left the roundtable, marking her official exit from the show, she and Rob also hugged, which didn’t make the final cut.
“They didn’t put that in,” Maura says. “I genuinely was like, ‘You know what, you smashed it.’ I gave him a hug, and I was like, ‘Enjoy.’”
Then, they had one more unaired interaction — “I tried to help you down the stairs and you said something smart,” Rob says.
But neither remembers exactly what she said.
“You could barely walk in that dress, so I was like, ‘Can I help you down the stairs?’” Rob says.
“But I looked great,” Maura adds. “And I won best dressed. And I’m getting a free Birkin, so, I won.”
(Appearing on TODAY Feb. 26, Rob confirmed to Jenna Bush Hager and Sheinelle Jones that he has “secured a Birkin.”)
Peacock and TODAY.com share a parent company, NBUniversal.

title: Pink Steps In to Guest Host ‘The Kelly Clarkson Show’ For This Special Reason
summary: "The Kelly Clarkson Show" has a guest host in Pink this week, March 2-6, in Clarkson's absence. Here's why.
story: "The Kelly Clarkson Show" has a special guest host this week: Pink.
The "Trustfall" singer is stepping into Kelly Clarkson's shoes for the week of March 2.
What inspired the timing of Pink's appearance and how can you tune in? Here's everything to know.
Who's Hosting 'The Kelly Clarkson Show' This Week?
Women's History Month began on Sunday, March 1. To kick off the month, "The Kelly Clarkson Show" is airing a week of special episodes from March 2 to March 6, hosted by Pink instead of Clarkson.
The episodes will feature conversations that “celebrate women’s achievements, resilience and impact across music, culture and community," NBC said in a press release.
Viewers can expect surprise performances and conversations with "trailblazers in entertainment" and "everyday people making a difference in their communities."
In a statement, Pink expressed her excitement for the job.

"I’ve played stadiums and danced on the side of buildings, but stepping in to guest host for my girl Kelly Clarkson is a brand new feat!" she said. "Let’s celebrate women, joy, resilience and whatever comes else comes our way!"
Clarkson also cheered on her friend in a statement.

"The only thing that bums me out about Pink hosting a week of ‘The Kelly Clarkson Show’ is that I’m not going to be able to be there to watch her," she said. "I have been a fan for so long and have always been inspired by her music and her person. I love her!"
How to Watch Pink's Episodes as Guest Host
"The Kelly Clarkson Show" airs weekdays on NBC. Pink will serve as a guest host between March 2-6.
Peacock subscribers can also stream episodes after they air. (Peacock and TODAY.com share a parent company, NBCUniversal.)
Kelly Clarkson Recently Announced That This Will Be the Final Season of Her Show
While appearing on TODAY on Feb. 20, Clarkson opened up about the end of "The Kelly Clarkson Show," which will conclude with Season 7 this fall.
During the interview, she told Carson Daly she made the decision with her children, 11-year-old daughter River Rose and 9-year-old son Remy, in mind.

“I think everybody probably gets the timing,” she said. “Our family life, the dynamic changed a bit, and it has changed for a minute now. You got kids, we’ve all got kids, and it’s one of those things when you kind of start seeing life as how precious it is, too. It’s like, ‘You know what, I’m really busy.’”
Clarkson's ex-husband and the father of her children, Brandon Blackstock, died of cancer in August 2025.
Clarkson emphasized that she didn't take the decision to end her show lightly, especially since she is close to staff.

“That’s what was really hard,” she said. “I work with a lot of people here, too. The crew’s been incredible, it wasn’t that the show isn’t doing well. That’s what kind of sucked, everything was going well, and that’s what was really hard."

title: Could Concert Tickets Get Cheaper? What the Trial of Live Nation-Ticketmaster Could Mean for You
summary: Some concertgoers are wondering if an antitrust lawsuit against Live Nation could spell relief for them the next time they're purchasing tickets to see their favorite artist.
story: Some concertgoers are wondering if an antitrust lawsuit against Live Nation could spell relief for them the next time they're purchasing tickets to see their favorite artist.

The Department of Justice's case against the entertainment giant began in court March 2. The government argues that the merger between Live Nation and Ticketmaster, which took place more than a decade ago, has allowed the company to operate a monopoly over the concert industry with its control of venues, promotion and ticketing.
Two years ago, the DOJ filed a complaint claiming that Live Nation now controls 80% or more of major concert venues’ primary ticketing for concerts plus a "growing" portion of ticket resales.
The DOJ alleges Live Nation has been able to tack on fees and increase prices for fans, describing it as "expansive violations of the antitrust laws."
What the Antitrust Lawsuit Could Mean for Fans
In recent years, music fans have become increasingly frustrated as concert ticket prices have soared, with tickets for some artists costing thousands of dollars on the resale market.

Rebecca Haw Allensworth, an antitrust expert and visiting professor at Harvard Law School, spoke with NBC’s Savannah Sellers about the DOJ's case against Live Nation and suggested that the government will likely "pursue a breakup."

“I think if they win this monopolization claim, they’re going to be arguing for some kind of breakup, either between the Live Nation arm and Ticketmaster or within Live Nation, maybe splitting off the promotion services from the venues," she said.
This means that for consumers, "the hope would be that you would have more options, lower prices, a better ticketing experience and less malfunctions of a ticketing system," she said.
The trial is expected to last around six weeks. If the government does win the case, it would likely be some time before consumers notice any differences while purchasing tickets.

Live Nation has denied that it has a monopoly in the market. The company told TODAY that 75% of all U.S. concert tickets are priced under $100. The company also said it is committed to “consumer-focused reforms” and “giving artists meaningful control over how their tickets are sold.”

Live Nation has previously faced scrutiny for its handling of ticket sales for high profile tours like Taylor Swift’s “Eras Tour.” In 2022, the singer spoke out about the messy ticket sales rollout by Ticketmaster.

“There are a multitude of reasons why people had such a hard time trying to get tickets and I’m trying to figure out how this situation can be improved moving forward. I’m not going to make excuses for anyone because we asked them, multiple times, if they could handle this kind of demand and we were assured they could,” she said at the time, in part.
This year alone, Live Nation will host some of this year’s hottest acts, including Lady Gaga and Harry Styles. Some artists are also  advocating for change in the ticketing industry.

Kid Rock, who has previously said he believes music fans and artists have been “getting screwed for for too long by the ticketing system,” has been named a potential witness in the trial against Live Nation.

Other potential witnesses include Minnesota Timberwolves CEO Matthew Caldwell, Roc Nation CEO Desiree Perez, Live Nation Entertainment CEO Michael Rapino and Mumford & Sons keyboardist Ben Lovett, per NBC News.

title: Inside Charles Leclerc and Alexandra Saint Mleux's Wedding
summary: Charles Leclerc and Alexandra Saint Mleux are officially husband and wife. Here are all the details about their Monaco wedding.
story: Charles Leclerc and Alexandra Saint Mleux are officially husband and wife.
The gorgeous couple confirmed their marriage on social media on March 2, sharing photos and videos from their civil ceremony.
The Formula 1 Ferrari driver revealed that they said “I do” on Feb. 28 in Monaco, also noting that a second celebration will take place next year.

“A day we’ll forever remember. Part one is done and part 2 will be next year with all of our close ones,” the athlete captioned his Instagram post filled with photos of the two in a bright red vintage Ferrari, with their dog Leo, and the ceremony.
Meanwhile, the newly-minted Alexandra Leclerc also shared details of her wedding dress and more.
“Dream 🪽Can’t wait to marry you all over again next year x,” the bride captioned her set of photos.
Here is everything to know about Charles and Alexandra Leclerc’s wedding day.
When and Where Did the Wedding Take Place?
Charles and Alexandra Leclerc’s wedding took place Feb. 28, 2026 in Monte Carlo, Monaco, according to the groom’s Instagram.
According to the newlyweds, they will have another wedding with all their “close ones” next year.
What Did Alexandra Wear?
In an Instagram post, the bride shared details of her wedding dress, jewelry, hair and makeup.

Her “absolute dream dress” was designed by Paolo Sebastian. The couture brand described the gown as a “French Chantilly lace fishtail gown featuring a scalloped neckline, delicately adorned with three-dimensional floral and butterfly embellishments, and finished with bespoke embroidered initials and their wedding date.”
Alexandra Leclerc wore jewelry by Graff. Her elegant updo was done by Laurie Zanoletti and Amalia Sarbut did her makeup.
Who Was Invited?
In attendance were Charles Leclerc’s brothers Arthur Leclerc and Lorenzo Leclerc and his wife, Charlotte. Their mother, Pascale Leclerc, was also seen in photos shared by the groom.
A number of guests are seen in the videos shared by the couple, but it’s unknown if any other Formula 1 drivers were in attendance.
Additional Details on Charles and Alexandra’s Wedding Day
The couple’s civil ceremony included floral arrangements filled with white lilies and what appears to be baby’s breath.
Their wedding cake was multi-layered and included a tiny miniature Leo cake topper on the side with a tiny “bite” taken from their pup.
Leo, their dachshund, was also in attendance and wore a black tuxedo.

title: Carole Radziwill Is Returning to ‘Real Housewives of New York City’ for Season 16
summary: Carole Radziwill is returning to “The Real Housewives of New York City” after an absence of more than seven years
story: Carole Radziwill is returning to “The Real Housewives of New York City” after an absence of more than seven years.
Radziwill will be featured as a "friend of" the cast in Season 16, which begins filming this week, Bravo announced March 2 in a press release and on social media.

"In the marathon of life, loyalty is everything. And because of Bravo fans’ loyalty, Carole Radziwill is returning to #RHONY as a friend-of for Season 16!!!" the network wrote alongside images of Radziwill on Instagram.

The author and journalist joined “RHONY” in Season 5. She left the series in 2018 after Season 10 amid reported feuds with executive producer Andy Cohen and cast member Bethenny Frankel, with whom Radziwill got into a heated argument during the show's Season 10 reunion.

Radziwill and Cohen have since made amends with Radziwill signaling the possibility of a return to “RHONY” in August 2025, when she made a surprise appearance on “Watch What Happens Live with Andy Cohen.”
She followed that by appearing at BravoCon 2025 in Las Vegas in November 2025.

Radziwill was married to the late filmmaker and television executive Anthony Radziwill, who was the first cousin of the late John F. Kennedy Jr. She wrote about her marriage with her late husband and their close bond with Kennedy and his wife, Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy, in her 2005 memoir “What Remains: A Memoir of Fate, Friendship and Love.”
Radziwill’s return to “RHONY” comes as interest in Kennedy and Bessette-Kennedy’s relationship has surged thanks to the Hulu series “Love Story,” which is based on their tragic romance.
"RHONY" cast members Sai De Silva, Erin Lichy and Jessel Taank will also return to the franchise for Season 16, Bravo confirmed.
They'll be joined by several new full-time cast members, including celebrity fitness trainer Erika Hammond, makeup artist Daisy Toye and public relations pro Hailey Glassman.
Season 15 cast members Brynn Whitfield, Ubah Hassan, Racquel Chevremont and Jenna Lyons have all exited the series as has Rebecca Minkoff, who was a “friend of” the cast last season.

title: All About the Shocking 'Paradise' Season 2 Twist With Annie and Her Baby
summary: The new "Paradise" Season 2, Episode 4, shows viewers what happened after Xavier was found by Annie, a survivor played by Shailene Woodley.
story: Warning: Spoilers ahead for “Paradise” Season 2, Episode 4
Season 2 of “Paradise” kicked off with a slew of new twists and questions to be answered.
The fourth episode, “A Holy Charge,” sees what happens after Sterling K. Brown’s Agent Xavier Collins is injured by a survivor and found by Shailene Woodley’s character Annie.
Annie is a former Graceland tour guide in Memphis, Tennessee, who survives the catastrophic events from Season 1 when the world seemingly ends.

Years later, a group of survivors run into her at the Elvis Presley estate. Its leader, Link (Thomas Doherty), and Annie develop a romantic relationship and after one night together she becomes pregnant. Link, however, doesn’t know of her pregnancy since he left the following day in search of the bunker in Colorado.

After she finds an injured Xavier, Annie, a former med student, helps heal him. He insists on traveling to Atlanta, Georgia, to search for his wife, Teri. Annie, however, tells him that he will take her to Colorado to find Link.

The two finally decide that they will travel in search of Teri, and then head to Colorado. As Xavier, on foot, and Annie, on horseback, head out on their journey, she starts to experience pregnancy complications.
They run into a group of people, who Annie warns are not their friends and to avoid contact.
Annie begins to feel worse and reveals to Xavier that she is suffering from preeclampsia, a pregnancy complication that can lead to serious if not fatal results for the mother and baby.
“I’m sorry, I thought if I told you you wouldn’t take me,” she tells Xavier, as they prepare for her to give birth.
An overwhelmed Xavier decides to go ask the group of people they passed for help despite Annie's pleas. She's worried that they will be dangerous and hurt them instead of helping them.
To Annie’s surprise, the whole group arrives just in time to deliver her baby girl.
Annie's health begins to decline and one of the women helping tells Xavier that the placenta won’t come out and the bleeding won’t stop.
“There’s nothing we can do,” Annie says, begging the women to stop helping her. “It's OK. No, I knew..."
She hands Xavier a note she had written for her baby.

“Xavier, give this to her when she’s old enough... Xavier, look at me,” she says as she’s dying. “It has to be you. Find her father. He was leading a group to the bunker to start the world. You will not let her be afraid of people. Promise me, Xavier. This is your holy charge. OK?”
Annie dies.
Xavier and the group bury her, and they offer to take the baby. He declines since he made Annie a promise.
Headed to Atlanta, he makes a stop and reads the letter Annie wrote to her daughter.

"Hello, darling. If you're reading this, I'm sorry that we never got the chance to meet. I don't know how old you'll be, or who you'll be with,” the letter reads. “All I know is that you were born into a hard world and I hope you can feel the love that I have for you, especially in your darkest moments and the longest nights.

“There are good people in this world,” the letter continues. “There always have been and there always will be. I trust that you’ve become one of them. I trust that love travels across death, across time. That it bridges impossible distances and binds people who’ve never known each other. I will always imagine you grown and brave and beautiful. I love you so much, my beautiful baby. Love, your mama.”

The episode ends with Link and his group arriving outside the bunker and sending a message to those inside. Xavier is also seen arriving in Atlanta, at the location where Teri sent out the radio broadcast from. He runs into a man who says he's a friend of Teri's and that she has been taken.

title: People Think the Jim Carrey That Attended the César Awards Is a Fake
summary: In what sounds like something more befitting of “The Mask” than real life, people online are claiming that there's a fake Jim Carrey out in the world.
story: In what sounds like something more befitting of “The Mask” than real life, people online are claiming that there's a fake Jim Carrey out in the world.
After the 64-year-old actor made an appearance at the César Awards last month, online conspiracies emerged about him being cloned or impersonated after his post award interview.
The rumors only intensified after a makeup artist claimed he pretended to be Carrey at the awards ceremony.
Why Do People Think Jim Carrey Was Cloned?

The 2026 César Awards, which honor the best in French film, were held in Paris on Feb. 26. Carrey, who has kept a low profile in recent years and whose last film was 2024’s “Sonic the Hedgehog 3,” gave a speech in French while accepting an honorary César Award.

After his speech, he spoke with reporters about writing and rehearsing for the moment. Many were confused by his calmer and emotional demeanor, as opposed to his funny faces and wacky character that he’s known for.

Many online suggested that he got botox or work done on his face, while others commented on the difference in his voice. Others wrote, “He’s just trolling people and awards as only he can" and “He gave up and started going with the flow.”
However, Carrey's team said he was indeed on hand at the ceremony.
“Jim Carrey attended the César Awards, where he accepted his Honorary Cesar Award,” his team said in a statement to TODAY.com.
Carrey's previous red carpet appearance was in November 2025 when he reunited with his former “How the Grinch Stole Christmas” co-star Taylor Momsen at the 2025 Rock and Roll Hall of Fame.
Additionally, Gregory Caulier, general counsel for the César Awards, told Variety in a statement that Carrey was in attendance.
“Jim Carrey’s visit has been planned since this summer. From the outset, he was extremely touched by the Academy’s invitation,” he said.

“Eight months of ongoing, constructive discussions. He worked on his speech in French for months, asking me about the exact pronunciation of certain words,” Caulier said. “He came with his partner, his daughter, his grandson and 12 close friends and family members. His longtime publicist accompanied him. His old friend Michel Gondry, who has made a film and two series with him, was there, and they were delighted to see each other again.”
Who Is Alexis Stone and Why Do People Think He Impersonated Jim Carrey?

Alexis Stone, known for transforming into famous celebs, stoked interest in the matter with an Instagram post on March 1 that went viral in which he claimed he had impersonated the “Ace Ventura” star.

“Alexis Stone as Jim Carrey in Paris,” he captioned the post, which featured two photos of what looks like Carrey’s face, along with a shot of a prosthetic mask and teeth, makeup and wig, with the Eiffel Tower in the background.
The post generated plenty of conversation.
“🎯” commented Katy Perry.
“Still suspicious button———>,” another skeptical user wrote.
“We demand proof of life from Jim Carrey !!!!” someone else commented.
Other people also chimed in to note that AI might've played a role in the matter.
“That’s ai we need proof and we need to see that Jim is well,” someone wrote.
“This is not real. The picture with the make-up kit is AI 100%,” another person commented.
TODAY.com reached out to Stone and the César Awards for comment, but have not heard back.

title: Jamie Lee Curtis Reunites with Daniel Radcliffe 25 Years After Playing His Mom in a Movie
summary: It’s been 25 years since Jamie Lee Curtis and Daniel Radcliffe played mother and son in a 2001 film called “The Tailor of Panama.”
story: It’s been 25 years since Jamie Lee Curtis and Daniel Radcliffe played mother and son in a 2001 film called “The Tailor of Panama.”
As fate would have it, they were both scheduled to appear live on TODAY on March 2, and they shared a long-awaited reunion in Studio 1A.

While speaking with Hoda Kotb, Curtis mentioned how the “Harry Potter” star played her son in the 2001 film. Hoda and Curtis then asked Radcliffe, who was standing nearby prepping for his own TODAY segment, to join the interview.
As he sat down on the couch, Radcliffe hugged Curtis.
“You guys have been hugging and everyone’s like, What’s the connection?” Hoda asked.
“I haven’t seen Daniel since, how old were you?” Curtis asked her former co-star.
“Ten,” Radcliffe said.
As footage from “The Tailor of Panama” appeared on the screen, Curtis recalled the experience of working together with Radcliffe.

“What I remember was, and I just told Daniel this story, we were in the swimming pool. Daniel had to wear a fake tan because he was very pale and we were supposed to live in Panama, and all the kids were in the pool. He couldn’t go in the pool because he had a fake tan. And I remember he was sitting by the pool and I looked up at him at one point and I went, ‘Wait, you know, you look like that kid who’s on the cover of that book (‘Harry Potter’),’” she said.

Curtis explained that this was before Radcliffe was cast in the role of Harry Potter and said his mother confirmed that the casting team had already reached out to them about the possibility of signing on for seven films.
“I don’t think I knew about it at this point. I think my mum was probably looking at you like, ‘No, no, no, don’t mention it to him,’” he said. “But yeah, so it was long before I was cast.”
At this point, Curtis took a moment to applaud Radcliffe for the trajectory of his career.

“And since then, he’s not only done ('Harry Potter'), but now he’s doing this work, this new work (in 'The Fall and Rise of Reggie Dinkins'). And so he reminds me of Michelle Williams, people who are committed to the art of what they do. She was on ‘Dawson’s Creek.’ Now she’s in that fantastic show ‘Dying for Sex,’” she said.
Curtis continued, saying, “Daniel’s now on Broadway singing, dancing, doing monologues, doing something very important.”
Radcliffe is appearing in the Broadway show “Every Brilliant Thing.”
Hoda then took the opportunity to give Curtis a bit of credit for inviting Radcliffe to share the spotlight with her.
“Can I just say something about Jamie? This was Jamie’s segment. You have a segment coming up,” she said to Radcliffe.
Radcliff then encouraged Curtis to talk about her TV show.
“Everybody, watch ‘Scarpetta,’” he said.
“Watch ‘Scarpetta,’ done,” Curtis said.

title: Michael J. Fox Calls His Kids His ‘Biggest Gift’ During Sweet Actor Awards Speech
summary: Michael J. Fox said acting is the reason he met his wife Tracy Pollan and they had their four kids during a speech at the Actor Awards.
story: Michael J. Fox knows acting not only made him a star — it also made him a family man.
The “Back to the Future” star gave a touching short speech during the “I am an actor” segment of the Actor Awards, formerly the SAG Awards, on March 1.
After Kristen Wiig, Teyana Taylor, Delroy Lindo and Kate Hudson gave brief speeches, Fox appeared on camera, as the audience responded with thunderous applause before he even said a word.

“Before I left school, I moved from Canada to L.A. to try to make it as an actor,” he began. “A teacher of mine told me, ‘Fox, you’re not going to be cute forever.’ I didn’t know what to say to that, so I said, ‘Maybe just long enough, sir.’”
He then reflected on catching his big break when he was cast as Alex P. Keaton on “Family Ties,” which not only made him a star, but also changed the trajectory of his personal life.

“After a few years of dumpster diving in L.A., I ended up on ‘Family Ties,’ where I received the biggest gift of my career: I met my wife, the actor Tracy Pollan, who played Ellen, my girlfriend, and she gave me four gifts, our kids, Schuyler, Aquinnah, Esme and Sam. Sometimes I like to remind them if it weren’t for acting, they wouldn’t be here,” he said.
The camera then panned out to his oldest child Sam, who was sitting next to the Emmy-winning star.
“By the way, he’s not an actor. He’s just my date,” Fox joked. “I’m Michael J. Fox and a dad, and I’m an actor.”

Fox, who has won a pair of Actor Awards (when they were the Screen Actors Guild Awards) has been married to Pollan since 1988. He was 29 when he was diagnosed with Parkinson’s disease. In 2020, he announced he was retiring from acting, but he has returned to appear in the current third season of “Shrinking.”

title: Seth Rogen Accepts Late Co-Star Catherine O’Hara’s Actor Award With Speech In Her Honor
summary: Seth Rogen shared a touching tribute to his late co-star Catherine O’Hara at the 2026 Actor Awards
story: Seth Rogen shared a touching tribute to his late co-star Catherine O’Hara at the 2026 Actor Awards.
O’Hara, who died at the age of 71 on Jan. 30, posthumously won the award for female actor in a comedy series for her role in Apple TV’s “The Studio,” which Rogen created and stars in.
At the Actor Awards, formerly known as the SAG Awards, Rogen took to the stage after O’Hara’s name was announced.

“I was asked to assume the very sad honor of accepting this award on O’Hara’s behalf. I know she would have been honored to receive this award from her fellow performers, who I know she respected so much. She was such big fans of all of yours,” he said.
Rogen then reflected on his experience working with the late actor while filming “The Studio.”

“Something that I’ve just been marveling at over the last few weeks was really her ability to be generous and kind and gracious while never, ever minimizing her own talents and her own ability to contribute to the work that we were doing,” he said. “She knew she could destroy, and she wanted to destroy every day on set.”
Rogen went on to share a story from the show’s set and explained how O’Hara would email him and co-creator Evan Goldberg before each day she was set to film the show.

“And it said, ‘Hello, I hope you’ll consider the following.’ And then there would be a completely rewritten version of the scene she was in. And literally 100% of the time, it made not just her character better, but it made the scene better and the entire show better as a whole,” he said.
Rogen then applauded O’Hara for not only her talent, but her gentle approach to working with other actors.
“And she really showed that you can be a genius and be kind and one of those things does not have to come at the expense of the other in any way, shape or form,” he said.
At the end of his speech, Rogen encouraged the audience to keep O’Hara’s memory alive.

“If you have people in your lives that don’t know her work, if they’re kids in your lives, or just people who are out of touch or stupid or something, just show them O’Hara dancing to Harry Belafonte in ‘Beetlejuice.’ Show them O’Hara hurting her knee in ‘Best in Show’ and doing that amazing thing where she hobbles around. And tell the people as they are laughing that that’s Catherine O’Hara, and we were lucky that we got to live in a world where she so generously shared her talents with us,” he said.

While listening to Rogen’s speech, many attendees of the awards show grew emotional. The camera panned to Jenna Ortega, who starred alongside O’Hara in “Beetlejuice Beetlejuice,” as she was wiping a tear away from her eyes.
On Feb. 9, NBC News confirmed that O’Hara died of a pulmonary embolism, or a blood clot in the lungs. Rectal cancer was the sequential cause of death.

title: Who is set to be in charge in Iran now that Khamenei is dead? A powerful hard-line military corps
summary: The U.S.-Israeli attack that ended Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s iron-fisted rule of Iran also created a power vacuum in the Islamic Republic for the first time in decades, with a group of clerics convening to cast their votes on who would succeed him.
story: The U.S.-Israeli attack that ended Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s iron-fisted rule of Iran also created a power vacuum in the Islamic Republic for the first time in decades, with a group of clerics convening to cast their votes on who would succeed him.

While front-runners to succeed Khamenei have emerged, including the assassinated supreme leader's second son, Mojtaba Khamenei, the real power is now likely to be in the hands of the heavily-armed force that propped up Khamenei for decades: the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.

“Even if they replace the supreme leader, what is left of the regime is the IRGC. And the IRGC is going to be the last vestige remaining of the regime until the regime is overhauled, either within itself or by external forces,” said Afshon Ostovar, an Iran expert at the Naval Postgraduate School in California and the author of “Vanguard of the Imam,” a history of the Revolutionary Guards.

“Once the smoke clears, if there’s not a complete regime change, then the people who will be in charge of Iran will be associated or the actual command of the IRGC,” he said, noting that his views are not the official position of the Department of Defense.

title: What we know about U.S. service members killed in the Iran war
summary: The U.S. government has said six U.S. service members have been killed in the military operation that started early Saturday with U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran.
story: The U.S. government has said six U.S. service members have been killed in the military operation that started early Saturday with U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran. Iran responded by launching missiles and drones in nearby countries.
At least five of the six U.S. service members, all of them Army reservists, died when a drone struck a port in Kuwait on Sunday, military officials said.
They were identified as Capt. Cody A. Khork, 35; Sgt. 1st Class Noah L. Tietjens, 42; Sgt. 1st Class Nicole M. Amor, 39; Spc. Declan J. Coady, 20; and Maj. Jeffrey O'Brien, 45.
A sixth soldier, Chief Warrant Officer Robert Marzan, is believed to have died at the scene of the attack, the Defense Department said.
Capt. Cody A. Khork
Khork, 35, was from Winter Haven, Florida. He enlisted in the National Guard in 2009 and was commissioned as a military police officer in the Army Reserve in 2014, the military said.
Khork's friend Abbas Jaffer posted photos of them together on Instagram.
"To my best friend, best man, and forever brother. I’m eternally grateful to have shared countless adventures, stories, and shenanigans with over the past 16 years," Jaffer said.
"You’re the kindest and best person I’ve ever known. There’s not enough words to describe how I feel," he added. "RIP my brother. Until we meet again."
Sgt. 1st Class Noah L. Tietjens
Tietjens, 42, was from Bellevue, Nebraska. Tietjens joined the Army Reserve as a wheeled vehicle mechanic in 2006.
Tietjens was a black belt in martial arts and was very dedicated to the craft, Julius Melegrito, one of the instructors at Martial Arts International in Bellevue, said in an interview.
Melegrito said that whenever Tietjens wasn't out on deployment, he was supporting his family's journey in martial arts and hanging out around the dojo.
Tietjens joined in 2008, and the family are all black belts now.
Melegrito described Tietjens as confident, respectful and easy to get along with. He said that when his wife met Tietjens, she said: "This is the kind of guy who always makes you feel welcome."
Tietjens would man the door at Martial Arts International events because of his "strict but friendly" nature, Melegrito said, calling Tietjens "the kind of person that gives."
"He died giving for his country," he said. Tietjens was "very well respected by others. He has that confidence, but he has that friendliness, as he deals with all different kinds of individuals."
Sgt. 1st Class Nicole M. Amor
Amor, 39, was from White Bear Lake, Minnesota. Amor joined the National Guard in 2005 and transferred to the Army Reserve in 2006, Army Reserve Command said. She deployed to Kuwait and Iraq in 2019.
Sen. Amy Klobuchar, D-Minn., said on X that Amor "made the ultimate sacrifice serving our country."
"Our hearts are with her family, loved ones, and all those in our armed forces," Klobuchar said.
Spc. Declan J. Coady
Coady, 20, was from West Des Moines, Iowa. Coady enlisted in the Army Reserve in 2023, the command said.
Coady was a student at Drake University in Des Moines. The school said on Facebook that it was "heartbroken" to learn of his death.
He was "a well-loved and highly dedicated Drake University student studying information systems, cybersecurity, and computer science," according to the post.

"He has bravely served in the U.S. Army Reserves since 2023 as an Information Technologies Specialist and had an incredibly bright future ahead of him," Coady's university said. "In moments like these, we must all come together to show much-needed love and compassion for the Coady family through this incredibly difficult time."
His sister Keira Coady described him Wednesday in a family statement as smart, kind, an avid gamer and a beloved member of ROTC at Drake University.
Coady died on his way to the hospital after he was injured in the airstrike in Kuwait, the statement said.

“On Sunday, March 1, after trying to check in, we heard nothing. Trying to be positive we all assumed he was just in a situation where he couldn’t message back, but we all knew something was wrong,” the statement said. “At 8 p.m. as we all were getting ready to go to bed, the doorbell rang, and the rest of that night will forever be one of the worst nights of our lives. While it’s all blurry, we all knew what the doorbell meant.”
Keira remembered her brother as reserved but passionate.
“He was supposed to be 21 on May 5. He was just a baby, and will forever be mine and Aidan’s baby brother, Rowan’s older brother, and our parents’ son,” she wrote.
“He was truly a rock in all of our lives and was just the most amazing brother and son my family could have asked for,” she added.

John Crandall, Coady’s uncle, remembered his nephew as an “incredibly gifted young man.” He was a former Eagle Scout who followed his older brother’s footsteps in joining the Army, with dreams of becoming an officer, Crandall told NBC’s “TODAY” show.
“There was just something beautiful about him, something amazing, something very intelligent about him,” he said. “He put himself in a position where many people will never go.”
Maj. Jeffrey O'Brien
O'Brien, 45, was from Indianola, Iowa, the Pentagon said.
Additional information was not immediately available.
Chief Warrant Officer 3 Robert Marzan

Marzan, 54, was from Sacramento, California. He is believed to have died at the scene of the drone attack, but medical officials are still awaiting positive identification from the medical examiner, the Pentagon said in a statement.
Additional information about a possible cause of death was not immediately available.
All six service members were assigned to the 103rd Sustainment Command based in Des Moines, military officials said.
In addition to those who died, 18 other U.S. service members have suffered serious injuries, a U.S. official told NBC News on Monday.

"We honor our fallen Heroes, who served fearlessly and selflessly in defense of our nation. Their sacrifice, and the sacrifices of their families, will never be forgotten,” Lt. Gen. Robert Harter, chief of Army Reserve and commanding general of U.S. Army Reserve Command, said in a statement.
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google
The U.S. and Israel launched attacks in Iran around 1:15 a.m. ET Saturday, using “precision munitions launched from air, land and sea” and one-way drones, Central Command has said.
Iran’s Red Crescent said nearly 950 people have been killed in Israeli and American strikes. Iran’s supreme leader, Ali Khamenei, was also killed.
Iran responded to the U.S. and Israeli strikes by launching missiles and drones at targets in surrounding nations. Deaths were reported in Israel, the United Arab Emirates, Kuwait and Bahrain.

Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said Tuesday that it launched 230 attack drones on a U.S. base in Erbil, Iraq, and the U.S. Ali Al Salem and Arifjan bases in Kuwait, the semiofficial Iranian news agency Tasnim reported.
The military action could last weeks, President Donald Trump said Monday. “We projected four to five weeks, but we have capability to go far longer than that,” he said.
Trump and other administration officials have said the U.S. attacked Iran because of an imminent threat and after nuclear talks failed to reach an agreement.

Some members of Congress have questioned that assessment and Trump’s power to attack Iran without seeking approval from Congress. Democrats and some Republicans plan to force a war powers vote that could restrict Trump’s ability to carry out further attacks in Iran.

title: Shipping slows through Strait of Hormuz, threatening to snarl global trade
summary: The Strait of Hormuz, normally jammed with oil tankers and cargo ships, has been nearly emptied by the war in the Middle East.
story: The Strait of Hormuz, normally jammed with oil tankers and cargo ships, has been nearly emptied by the war in the Middle East. Ongoing strikes by the U.S. and threats from Iran to fire on ships have made the risks of navigating the vital waterway for the global supply chain no longer worth the rewards.

Tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday had dropped around 90% compared with last week, according to ship tracker MarineTraffic, as oil and gas tankers cluster just outside of the strait.

title: Inside the underground nerve center of Israel's first responders
summary: NBC News visited the main nerve center for Israel’s primary emergency service, Magen David Adom, as the country was on stand by for retaliatory drone strikes.
story: TEL AVIV — The main nerve center for Israel’s primary emergency service could resemble any dispatch hub in any American city — a hive of uniformed first-responders surrounded by ceiling-height monitors and an expanse of computers.

But Magen David Adom’s dispatch unit in Ramla, about 12 miles southeast of Tel Aviv, is more than 100 feet underground, safeguarded by thick walls and a sophisticated respirator system capable of providing clean air in case of conventional and nonconventional attacks.

“You wouldn’t imagine any other emergency services, civilian emergency services, in the world working in a shelter. But for us, this is a need, a basic need,” said Uri Shacham, MDA’s deputy director and chief of staff. The MDA’s role, he said, was “to make sure that no matter what happens outside, no matter how challenging the situation, this brain actually continues to function.”

When NBC News visited the facility Tuesday, the mood seemed busy but relaxed as about a dozen uniformed dispatchers handled phone calls and plotted routes for emergency vehicles on an array of screens.
Soon enough, phones blared with news of incoming projectiles from Iran. The alert seemed to arrive on civilian phones just as quickly as it came to the attention of the dispatchers.
Within minutes, a dispatcher’s screen showed green ovals indicating the likely destination of the missiles.
At first, two or three covered most of the greater Tel Aviv area, Israel’s largest metropolitan region covering around 586 square miles and home to more than 3.9 million residents.
As the missiles approached, the ovals turned orange then red and fragmented into more than a dozen smaller ovals as software narrowed down their likely paths.
A separate screen showed a city map and the location of what the system had identified as potential fallen debris or a missile impact.

The map showed ambulances already on their way to the site, though the dispatcher never picked up the phone because the information traveled automatically from the military through the dispatcher and on to nearby ambulances and motorcycle medics.

“In the past, if I would receive the call of a burning house due to a missile fall, they would have to call me and say, listen, there is a fire, send your ambulance,” Shachem said. “Now we work on the same computerized system. And once they put in their system — a fire in Tel Aviv in this location because of a suspected missile hit — it will automatically be sent to Magen David Adom, sparing time, sparing any info detail that is lost during translation.”
The highly sophisticated system seems to stretch the limits of just how far human error can be reduced.
Yet, on the sharp end of all that inhuman infallibility, there are still paramedics like Itai Orion, who counts himself lucky for not yet having been called out to the scene of a missile strike.
But his wife’s family lives in Beit Shemesh, where a direct hit Sunday killed nine people huddled in a bomb shelter — the largest single death toll of any strike since Iran’s counterattack began.
